calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-14-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-13-2019 Continued To Jul-03-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Request For Default Judgment In Favor Of Plaintiffs
Off Calendar May-16-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Blue Chip Realty Fund, LLC.
Dept. 514. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703. Email: mlyon@sftc.org.

Edward E. Smith, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Charles Cunningham, who was placed under oath and testified. Mr. Smith also appeared for plaintiff Olivia Cunningham, who was not present for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Blue Chip Realty Fund, LLC.
Plaintiffs' complaint for Quiet Title, Declaratory Relief and Cancellation of Instrument was filed on December 5, 2017.
Proof of Service of Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents was filed on February 19, 2019.
Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents were served on defendant via Notice & Acknowledgment filed January 7, 2019.
Default as to defendant Blue Chip Realty Fund, LLC was entered on February 19, 2019.
DOE Defendants were dismissed on March 15, 2019.
Dismissal without prejudice as to plaintiffs' 1st Cause of Action (Quiet Title) and 2nd Cause of Action (Declaratory Relief) ONLY, was filed today, May 16, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Charles Cunningham and Olivia Cunningham, and against the Defaulted Defendant Blue Chip Realty Fund, LLC, as follows:

As to the Third Cause of Action in plaintiffs' complaint, Judgment is entered in favor of plaintiffs Charles and Olivia Cunningham, and against defendant Blue Chip Realty Fund, LLC, cancelling that certain Deed of Trust between Charles Cunningham and Olivia Cunningham, as borrowers, and JSW Financial, Inc. a California corporation, as beneficiary recorded on March 14, 2006, so that the Deed of Trust no longer remains as a lien against plaintiffs' real property located at 599 Oak Park Drive, San Francisco, CA.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
